llmWiki.unavailable503: HTTP 503: LLM Wiki is not wired in this server process (not wiki.read; missing capability returns 403).
llmWiki.pickFromVault: Pick a note from the vault tree to preview.
llmWiki.searchEmpty: No results yet. Run a search after entering a substring.
llmWiki.wireInspectJson: No automatic hints; inspect the JSON below (check python_executable vs your pip/poetry interpreter, crate_file location, plugins_config_path).

scheduledJobs.title: Scheduled jobs
scheduledJobs.hint: List persisted jobs, inspect recent runs, and trigger manual executions when permitted.
scheduledJobs.selectJob: Select a job to view runs.
scheduledJobs.noRuns: No runs recorded for this job yet.
scheduledJobs.editFullHint: Trigger, payload, session binding, and budget apply on save (same validation as create).

contract.violationDetail: Some items are missing required correlation fields.
contract.ob03NoTraceBody: This run has timeline events but no trace_id was found in payloads. External links and trace-based triage may be incomplete—verify server audit payloads (PRD Ob-03).
contract.auditPayloadDetail: Some events are missing correlation keys in payload (e.g. trace_id). Cross-navigation may be degraded.

approval.runLinkAria: Open run detail for this approval

policyRelease.wizardHelp: Signature uses the same canonical JSON as the Python signer: sorted keys, no extra spaces, HMAC-SHA256 over {"version","policy_document"}. Matches AGENTIUM_POLICY_RELEASE_HMAC_SECRET on the server.
policyRelease.hmacSecret: HMAC secret (local only — do not commit)
policyRelease.invalidJsonDocument: policy_document must be valid JSON object
policyRelease.invalidJsonMetadata: metadata must be valid JSON object
policyRelease.secretRequired: HMAC secret is required to sign
policyRelease.selectRelease: Select a release from the table first.
policyRelease.tenantsRequired: Enter at least one tenant_id to activate.

evalGates.overallFail: One or more gates failed — not releasable

overview.failedRunsHint: Filtered when last audit event type contains fail/blocked/error.

policyChain.effectiveHint: Read-only bundle from GET /v1/policy/effective. Per-request outcomes remain in audit events (e.g. policy_decision).
policyChain.timelineHint: Filter audit events; use policy_decision with an optional run_id to align with the snapshot.

compliancePage.hint: Server uses exact event_type match; adjust to known audit labels from your deployment.

workflowIde.enterRunId: Enter a run_id to load GET /v1/workflows/{run_id}.
workflowIde.noSpecNodes: Server returned a workflow payload but spec_nodes is missing or empty—no DAG to draw (C-01 partial).
workflowIde.apiNote: Read-only graph from the control plane; compare with GET /v1/task-graph/{run_id} if configured.

background.stopConfirm: Stop the background daemon? This is idempotent but halts active work.
background.statusHint: Parsed hints from status JSON (when present).

governancePage.intro: Domain packs are loaded server-side from configured directories. There is no binary download API in the control plane yet—use repository docs and your deployment manifest.
governancePage.manifestFile: Pack root must include domain_pack.yaml (see DomainPackLoader).
governancePage.envHint: Paths and signing are configured via your runtime settings / secret store—not exposed here.

manifest.optionalHint: When set, must be a JSON object aligned with server RunManifest policy. Leave empty to omit. Errors such as missing manifest or SHA mismatch surface as API JSON (RM-02)—use the response detail below.
manifest.invalidJson: run_manifest must be valid JSON object or empty

toolsCatalog.title: Tool catalog
toolsCatalog.hint: Read-only list from GET /v1/tools (tools.read). Use Turn debug or Workspace to invoke tools.

sessionsPage.title: Sessions
sessionsPage.hint: Recent runs from GET /v1/runs/recent. Open workspace to send turns on a run_id, or inspect the timeline.
sessionsPage.needCap: Capability runs.read is required to list sessions.

workspace.title: Session workspace (Agent)
workspace.historyDeleteConfirm: Delete this chat session? It will be removed from the list.
workspace.memoryPluginHint: Per-session lane when the server exposes both backends (plugins.memory.optional_mem0_lane or primary mem0 with native SQLite fallback). If Mem0 is unavailable, the server falls back with an audit log line.
workspace.memoryPanelDisabled: Set a session ID and ensure you have chat.messages.read to load memory.
workspace.memoryPanelEmpty: No MID/LONG rows yet; with semantic extraction enabled the model writes declarative memories (extracted_memory). The chat column keeps raw turns (short-term). Set AGENTIUM_CHAT_MID_SEMANTIC_MEMORY=0 to disable.
workspace.skillHint: Changing skill updates the system context (optional SKILL.md excerpt). If the session row already exists, the UI PUT-syncs skill before the next send.
workspace.skillOrderHint: Check skills to bind; order is primary → secondary top-to-bottom. Use ↑↓ to reorder.
workspace.personaTemplateHint: Templates populate Identity · Soul · Tools · User text areas; edit freely afterward. Add roles server-side via AGENTIUM_PERSONA_TEMPLATES_DIR.
workspace.toolsAllowlistHint: Low-risk tools only; execution still follows server policy and the chat tool loop allowlist.
workspace.toolsNeedCapability: Listing tools requires tools.read (included on default user role).
workspace.agentToolsHint: Off by default; requires AGENTIUM_CHAT_AGENT_TOOLS on the server. Model may emit tool_calls; approval interrupts return 503 pending_tool_approval.
workspace.streamWorkbenchHint: Unavailable while Agent tools are enabled.
workspace.sessionMissingRunId: Fill in a session ID (run_id) before sending.
workspace.chatPendingToolApprovalHint: A tool round requires approval: resolve it in the approval hub and retry; approval_id in the response identifies the gate.
workspace.deepseekHint: Configure AGENTIUM_DEEPSEEK_API_KEY on the server and restart the control plane.
workspace.needCaps: Workspace requires chat.sessions.manage and chat.messages.send. Refresh GET /v1/me from Settings.
workspace.assistantPending: Pending approval — resolve in approval hub.
workspace.autoIngressLabel: Infer disposition / MCP tier from message (auto ingress)
workspace.autoIngressHint: Off: send explicit collect / followup / steer (no server-side classification from content).
workspace.ingressQueuedGeneric: Queued — session busy; message will run when the current turn finishes.
workspace.memoryFootnote: This panel calls GET …/memory?layers=mid,long; MID rows include Mem0-style LLM-extracted facts (extracted_memory) when AGENTIUM_CHAT_MID_SEMANTIC_MEMORY is on. SHORT excerpts stay server-side; chat column is raw turns.

settings.runManifestRm01: When issuing a turn, paste the same JSON as run_manifest in advanced options on Turn debug or Workspace.
settings.applyOk: Profile refreshed from GET /v1/me; capabilities updated.
settings.applyNeedIdentity: Fill tenant + user (header mode) or bearer token, then click again.
settings.apiBaseHint: For local dev, leave this empty: requests use same-origin /v1 and the Vite proxy (identity headers stay reliable). If you set an absolute URL like http://127.0.0.1:8765, the browser calls the control plane cross-origin; without CORS allowing X-Tenant-Id / X-User-Id / X-Role you may see missing_identity_headers or failed fetches.
settings.observabilityCorsHint: If you still see missing_identity_headers with tenant/user filled: clear API base to use the proxy, or enable CORS on the control plane.
settings.applyNetworkFailed: "Failed to fetch" usually means the browser never got an HTTP response. Check: (1) control plane is listening (e.g. agentium serve); (2) prefer leaving API base empty so requests hit same-origin /v1 (Vite proxy)—direct absolute URLs need working CORS; (3) Vite proxy target matches AGENTIUM_CONTROL_PLANE_URL / vite.config.
